title: Fix latch inference in a mux
prompt: The following Verilog infers a latch. Rewrite it so the output is fully assigned for all paths.
explanation: Combinational outputs must be assigned on every path.

title: Swap registers safely
prompt: Two registers are swapped on a clock edge. Use correct RTL style.
explanation: Non-blocking assignments avoid race-like behavior in clocked logic.

title: Design modulo-10 counter
prompt: Write a synchronous modulo-10 counter with active-low reset and enable.
explanation: State wraps after 9 and updates only when enable is asserted.

title: Sequence detector FSM
prompt: Implement an overlapping sequence detector for 101 using a Moore FSM.
starterCode:
typedef enum logic [1:0] {S0, S1, S2, S3} state_t;
// TODO
solution: Use separate next-state and output logic; output asserted only in detected state.
explanation: Interviewers care more about state transitions and overlap handling than syntax perfection.

title: 2-flop synchronizer
prompt: Write RTL for synchronizing a single-bit control signal into a destination clock domain.
solution:
always @(posedge clk_dst or negedge rst_n) begin
  if (!rst_n) begin sync1 <= 1'b0; sync2 <= 1'b0; end
  else begin sync1 <= async_in; sync2 <= sync1; end
end
explanation: This is the canonical answer for a 1-bit control crossing.

title: RTL Drill #6
prompt: Practice problem 6: debug or design a small RTL block and explain trade-offs.
solution: Reference approach: state assumptions, write synthesizable RTL, then discuss corner cases.
explanation: Use this as a whiteboard-style practice drill.
